prompt: Describe a place you've been to that no longer exists. What detail do you remember most clearly, and why that one?
Write about a time you changed your mind about a person. What specifically shifted — what you saw, or what you understood about yourself?
Recall a smell that drops you instantly into a specific year of your life. What was happening then that you couldn't have named at the time?
Pick one object in your home you'd recognize blindfolded. Why has that one accumulated more meaning than the rest?
Think of a conversation you overheard but were never part of. What about it has refused to leave you?
Write down something a teacher once said offhand that you're still thinking about. Have you ever told them?
There is a memory you've been quietly misremembering for years. Walk into it now and notice what you got wrong.
Describe a song that has nothing musical going for it but means everything to you because of when you heard it.
The last time you ate at your favorite restaurant before it closed, you didn't know it was the last time. Reconstruct that meal.
What photograph do you wish existed but doesn't? Describe it as if pinning it to a wall.
Name an apology you never delivered. Write it now, even if you wouldn't send it.
The first time you understood a joke an adult had made, when you were a child — what was the joke and how old were you when the meaning landed?
Choose a book you abandoned. Describe the moment you decided you were done with it.
Have you had the same dream more than once? Walk into it without trying to interpret it.
Someone you met exactly once stayed with you. Describe them in as much detail as you can summon.
Pick a stranger whose habit you have envied. What about it draws you in?
There is a building you know well that makes a particular sound at a particular time of day. Capture it.
Describe the quality of light in one room you used to spend time in. Don't describe the room itself, only the light.
What small inefficiency do you watch people do every day without correcting? Why has it earned your tolerance?
Notice the way a specific person you know holds their phone. What does the angle say about them?
Imagine you had to lose one of your senses for exactly one year, then get it back unchanged. Which would you sacrifice, and what would you do during the year?
If you could enforce one rule on every conversation you have for a week, what would it be? What would suffer because of it?
Invent a holiday. Who is it for, what does it require, and why doesn't it exist already?
For 24 hours, gravity is half its current strength. What changes about your day that nobody else seems to notice?
Pick a decision you almost made differently. Describe the version of you who made the other choice.
You're allowed one question to which you'd receive an honest answer from anyone, living or dead. What's the question, and who do you ask?
Describe an object that should exist but doesn't. Sketch it in words.
If you could require every adult to study one subject for a year, what would it be? What's the smallest reason you chose it?
Recall the taste of something you ate exactly once. Why didn't you go back for more?
Describe a sound you'd assign a color to if forced. What's the sound, and what color?
Some memories have a temperature. Pick one and describe it without naming the season.
Name a texture that calms you for no reason you can explain. When did you first notice this?
Describe a familiar place at 3 AM by sound alone, no visuals.
Write about a trait you faked until it became real. Did anyone notice the seam?
There's a skill you wish you'd never learned. What is it and how did learning it change you?
Describe a version of yourself that only one specific person has seen. Why them?
You grew into a role uncomfortably. Walk back to the moment you noticed you fit it.
Name a thing you say you hate but secretly enjoy. What's the cost of admitting it?
Pick a self-description you've outgrown. What did you replace it with?
List one thing you do because of who you used to be, not who you are now. Is it worth keeping?
Describe a friendship that ended without rupture. Where did the slack first show?
Whose absence did you notice only weeks after it was permanent? What signaled it?
Name a debt you owe that nobody is tracking. What stops you from settling it?
The shortest important conversation of your life — reconstruct it line by line.
There's a grudge you still hold whose origin you've forgotten. What does the grudge feel like in your body?
Recall a small favor someone did that became disproportionately useful. Have you returned it in any form?
Someone is right about you in a way that bothers you. Write it from their angle, fairly.
There's a skill you assumed you'd master quickly that took years instead. What did the long way teach you?
Describe the tool you reach for first when you sit down to do your work. Why that one?
Capture a failure in your work that nobody else has ever noticed. Has the cost shown up later?
What work are you most proud of that no one will ever see? Describe it for someone who wasn't there.
Name a compromise you made in your craft that you do not regret. What did the trade buy you?
Pick a task most people consider tedious that you find meditative. What does your mind do while your hands work?
Pick a room you spent hundreds of hours in once and have not returned to. Walk a perimeter in writing.
There's a hallway in your past with a particular feeling. Describe the feeling first, then guess at the cause.
Name a city you've never visited but think about often. What pulls you to it?
If you had unlimited money to buy one specific piece of land — not for building, just to own — where and why?
Describe the geography of a route you used to walk daily and no longer do. Who lives along it now?
Capture a house by its smell alone. Whose house is it, and when did you last go in?
Recall a thirty-minute window that felt much longer than it was. What was time doing then?
There's an hour of the day you most belong to. Describe yourself inside it.
If you could cut one full year out of your life and keep what came after, which year? What did you survive?
At what age did you feel most yourself? What had not yet happened?
Name a single ordinary day you would live again exactly as it was. What makes it worthy?
Recall a risk you didn't take that worked out anyway. Did the avoidance feel earned or lucky?
Recall a text thread that ended on a strange note. Read it back in your head and write what you wished you'd sent.
Name an event you skipped that changed nothing. Are you still glad you skipped it?
Describe an object you bought and threw out within a year. What were you trying to become when you bought it?
A friendship ended over something small. Have you ever measured the disproportion between the cost and the cause?
You discovered something significant about someone close to you. How long had you been around it without seeing it?
A skill you trained for years turned out to have less use than you expected. What did training in it actually teach you?
Describe a reaction of your own that surprised you. What did you find out about your wiring?
Something turned out to be much easier than you'd feared. Did the relief carry any disappointment?
Name a belief you hold but don't act on. Where in your day does the gap show?
There's a trait you criticize in others that you also have. When do you catch yourself?
Pick an opinion you'd defend in public but quietly doubt. What would loosen your grip on it?
Describe an aesthetic you publicly reject and privately admire. Where would you display it if there were no witnesses?
What's the funniest thing a child has ever said to you? Why did it land?
Recall a misunderstanding that escalated absurdly. At what point could it have been cleared up?
Pick an inside joke you share with one other person. Why is it funny only to the two of you?
Describe a situation that wasn't funny while it happened but is funny now. What changed?
Pick a piece of advice you ignored. What would have happened if you'd taken it?
Describe an envy you've had for someone whose life looked, on paper, less appealing than yours. What did they have that you wanted?
There's a moment you suspect was the high point of something. What followed it that confirmed it?
List three small rituals you didn't know you had until someone pointed them out. Where did they come from?
Imagine writing a short letter to your past self at age sixteen — not to console, just to inform. What single fact do you give them?
If your week were a film, what genre would the editor choose? Where would they cut?
Describe yourself in third person for one paragraph, as if a stranger had been observing you for a day.
Name something you understand now that would have made your twenties easier. Why couldn't you understand it then?
There's a question you've been quietly asking yourself for months. Write it down without trying to answer it.
Describe the most useful argument you've ever had. What got resolved that wouldn't have without conflict?
Pick a job you did badly. What part of you was being asked for that you couldn't supply?
There's a particular silence that means something specific to you. Describe both — the silence and the meaning.
Walk through your home and find the oldest object you own. Where did it come from, and why has it survived everything else?
Name someone you'd be embarrassed to bump into now. What would the embarrassment actually be about?
Recall a meal you remember more vividly than the people you ate it with. What was on the plate?
Describe the first time you realized your parents were people. What gave them away?
There's a question you wish you'd asked your grandparents. Ask it now into the air.
Write about a place you only know through someone else's story. What have you filled in?
Pick an heirloom you don't want. What's the polite way out, and why haven't you taken it?
Describe a friendship that survived a betrayal. What did the repair cost you?
Name a generosity you committed without anyone observing. Why did anonymity matter?
Recall a stranger who helped you and disappeared. What would you say if you saw them in line at a store today?
Pick a lie you told that turned out to be accidentally true. What did chance teach you about your own honesty?
Describe a near-cruelty you almost said aloud and didn't. What stopped your tongue?
Choose an unread book on your shelf. Why did you buy it, and what's keeping you from opening it?
There's a film you've seen too many times. What does each rewatch dig up that the last didn't?
Pick a song you used to love and now skip. What did you outgrow — the song or yourself?
Describe a piece of art that has confused you for years. What does the confusion offer that clarity wouldn't?
Name an album that's tied to one specific season. Listen to it inside that season in your head.
There's a phrase you say constantly without thinking. Where did you pick it up?
Pick a word that you find slightly disgusting for no clear reason. Why does it bother you?
Describe how you'd explain the internet to your great-grandmother, with no metaphors involving paper.
Name a foreign language you wish you spoke. What would you say first, and to whom?
There's a typo you make repeatedly. What does your hand know that your mind doesn't?
Pick a weather pattern that feels personal to you. When does it show up, and what does it ask?
Describe the longest you've ever stared at one thing without speaking. What were you waiting for?
There's a distance — exact in miles — that you think of as far. Why that number?
Name a stretch of road you could drive blindfolded. Why has it become muscle memory?
Pick a specific window — yours or someone else's — that you've spent time looking out of. What's the view, and why does it matter?
Recall a warning you ignored that turned out to be correct. What kind of person gave the warning?
Describe a habit you broke and don't miss. How did you replace the time?
Name a habit you broke and quietly miss. What did it do for you that the replacement doesn't?
There's a piece of clothing you wear like armor. What does it protect you from?
Pick an object you'd grab from a burning house second — not first, second. Why that one?
Describe a pet you didn't get. What would its name have been?
Recall the moment you realized an animal could be afraid. Whose animal, and what scared it?
There's a wild creature you've stared at for too long. What did it do or fail to do?
Pick a houseplant you killed. Reconstruct the timeline of its death.
Describe the difference between two trees you walk past regularly. Have you ever told anyone the difference?
Name an institution — a school, an office, a gym — whose smell you'd recognize anywhere. Where would you not want to smell it again?
Describe a specific corner in a specific bar. What's it like when no one is sitting there?
There's a public bench you've sat on more than once. Who else uses it?
Pick the strangest thing you've found on the street. Did you keep it?
Recall a place that gave you the feeling of being watched. Was anyone there?
Imagine the world's electricity cut for a week. Walk through your day three days into the blackout.
If you could relive any year of your life with one piece of foreknowledge, which year and what knowledge?
You can put one item in a time capsule for one specific person to find in fifty years. What goes in?
If your life had a soundtrack, what would the opening track be? What does it imply about what's coming?
Describe the last hour you'd want to live, without trying to make it heroic.
Pick a meal someone made for you that you've never replicated. Why hasn't it worked out of their kitchen?
Describe a recipe you've made enough times that you no longer measure. What did the proportions become for you?
Name a food you used to dislike that you now love. Where did the change happen?
Recall a dish from a country you've never visited. How did it find you?
There's a beverage that's tied to one specific person in your mind. What's the drink and who?
Pick a bad piece of advice you gave that turned out fine. What does that say about advice in general?
Describe the worst-paid hour of work you've ever done. Was there anything redeeming about the hour itself?
Name a small skill — useful only to you — that took unreasonable effort to acquire. Why was it worth it?
Recall a meeting that should have been an email. How long have you been forgiving the person who held it?
Pick a procrastination technique of yours. Be honest about whether it's actually working against you.
Describe a scene where a child taught you something accidentally. What were they doing?
There's a dynamic between you and a sibling — or a sibling you didn't have — that you can summarize in one image. What's the image?
Pick a relative whose life you've simplified in your head. What detail are you ignoring?
Recall the first funeral you remember. What were you wearing, and who told you to wear it?
Describe the most ordinary moment you can remember from being twelve. What did your hands do?
Pick an ambition you no longer have. When did it stop showing up in your daydreams?
There's a quiet pride you take in something most people would consider trivial. Defend it in writing.
Name a praise that landed wrong. Why didn't it match how you felt about your own work?
Recall a competition you lost. What did losing teach that winning would have hidden?
Describe the realization that a peer was better than you at something you cared about. What did you do with that?
Pick a public figure you used to admire and no longer do. What broke first — them or your image of them?
Describe a stranger you saw doing something kind. Why has it stuck?
Name a group of people you used to belong to and no longer do. What part of you stayed with them?
Recall an opinion you've held secretly for years. What would it cost to say it out loud?
There's a stance you take that nobody you know shares. What sustains it?
Pick a fear that's smaller now than it used to be. What shrank it?
Name a fear that's grown without your noticing. When did you last test it?
Describe a phobia you find embarrassing. Walk into the embarrassment.
Recall the first nightmare you remember. What's still recognizable about it now?
There's a specific anxiety that surfaces only in airports. Describe it.
Pick an unfinished project. What's the actual obstacle, not the one you tell people about?
Describe your relationship to your own handwriting. Do you like it?
There's a number you use as a password fragment. What does the number mean to you?
Recall a piece of correspondence — physical or digital — that you reread sometimes. What pulls you back?
Name something you've kept that nobody else would understand the value of. Defend the keeping.
Pick a scene where you thought you were being watched and weren't. What were you performing?
Describe an audience you imagine when you talk to yourself. Who is in the room?
There's a version of your voice you use only on the phone. Where did it come from?
Recall the last time you laughed alone. What set it off?
Name a private joke you have with no one. Tell it.
Pick a feature of your face you've made peace with. How long did the peace take?
Describe a feature you've never made peace with. Be specific about what bothers you.
There's a body part you take for granted. Pay it attention for a paragraph.
Recall the first time you saw yourself on video. What didn't match?
Name a piece of clothing you wore at a turning point. What survived the turning?
Pick a hobby you abandoned and don't miss. What was the wrong fit?
Describe a hobby you abandoned and do miss. What's stopping you from picking it back up?
There's an interest you've kept secret because it doesn't fit your image. Confess it on paper.
Name a curiosity you've had for years and never pursued. What's the cost of the not-pursuing?
Recall a class you took that did nothing for you at the time and pays off now. Where does it show up?
Pick a stranger you'll never see again whose face you remember. What about it stayed?
Describe a face from a dream you can't place. Whose features did your mind borrow?
There's a specific gesture that means something only to you. Describe it.
Recall a handshake you remember. Whose, and what did it tell you?
Name the smallest physical detail of a person you love. Why that one?
Pick a noise that lives in your body. Where in your body does it live?
Describe a quiet that scared you. What was waiting underneath it?
There's a sound you make when you're alone that you'd never make in front of anyone. What does it mean?
Recall a song you couldn't get out of your head for a week. What was it asking of you?
Name a piece of music that makes you walk faster. Why does it move you literally?
Pick a season you used to love that you've cooled on. What changed?
Describe a season that arrived too early or too late this year. What did the misalignment do?
There's a year you'd describe as a corridor — long, narrow, only one direction. Which year?
Recall a New Year's Eve that mattered. What was at midnight?
Name a birthday that landed strangely. Why?
Pick a meal you ate alone in public. Was it nourishing or performative?
Describe a stretch of solitude you've revisited mentally many times. What's its half-life?
There's a place you go when you need to be unreachable. How do you know when you need it?
Recall a long stretch of being alone that broke you down or built you up. Which way did it run?
Name a person whose company feels like solitude. What does that mean to you?
Pick an injury that healed wrong. What does it tell you when the weather changes?
Describe a sickness you don't want to forget. What did it slow you down to see?
There's an instance your body knew something before your mind did. Describe the lag.
Recall the last time you were genuinely well rested. What was different about the day before?
Name a physical sensation you wish you could bottle. When does it arrive?
Pick an argument you replay in your head. What new line do you give yourself each time?
Describe a silence in a relationship that did more work than the words around it.
There's a phrase your family overuses. What does it cover for?
Recall an instance of forgiveness. Whose was it and how did you know it had happened?
Name a misunderstanding that defined a relationship for you. Has it ever been corrected?
Pick a piece of furniture you sat on for years. Where is it now?
Describe a doorway you've passed through hundreds of times. What's specific about it?
There's a corner of your home that nobody else notices. What lives there?
Recall the first place you ever lived alone. What did the silence sound like?
Name a building you'd protect from demolition if you could only save one. Why that one?
There's a stranger you sat next to on a long flight or train ride. What did you not ask them?
Describe a meal at someone else's family table where you understood you were a guest. What signaled it?
Name a small, persistent inconvenience in your home. Why haven't you fixed it?
Pick the most expensive thing you've ever broken. What did breaking it cost beyond the price?
Recall an item you bought specifically to impress someone. Did it work?
There's a sound your body makes that your past self would have been mortified by. Walk into the surrender.
Describe a piece of mail you've kept. What's it doing in your life now?
Name something you've gotten worse at as you've aged. Has it freed up anything?
Pick a chore you secretly like. What does liking it tell you about yourself?
Recall a stretch of work where you forgot to check the time. What were you doing?
There's a stretch of road or street that feels lucky to you. Why have you decided that?
Describe the most useful boredom you've ever had. What did boredom let you find?
Name a household appliance you've spoken to. Why did you?
Pick a smell from your childhood you'd avoid as an adult. What did you tolerate that you can't anymore?
Recall a teacher you disliked who turned out to be right. What did they see in you?
Walk through your wallet or bag and inventory items you don't need. Why are they still there?
Describe a kindness you witnessed and never told anyone about. Why didn't you?
There's a gift you received and resented receiving. Why?
Pick a stranger's dog you remember. What about that particular animal?
Name an animal you've been afraid of in a way that surprised you. What was it doing?
Recall a haircut that changed something. What was it that changed?
Describe an outfit you wore exactly once. Where is it now?
There's a thing you wear to feel invisible. What does it look like?
Pick the most uncomfortable shoes you ever loved. What did you put up with for them?
Recall a piece of jewelry that wasn't valuable but mattered. Where is it now?
Name a hairstyle you regret. Defend it from the inside of the era it belonged to.
Describe a meal you cooked that flopped spectacularly. Was the flop entirely your fault?
Pick a kitchen tool you didn't know you needed until you used it. What changed?
Recall the first time you cooked for someone you wanted to impress. What did you make?
There's a flavor you can't replicate from your childhood. What's the closest you've gotten?
Name a beverage your morning depends on. What happens if it's missing?
Describe a meal that felt like an apology. Whose was it?
Pick a meal that felt like a conversation. What did it say?
Recall a small splurge you don't regret. What about it earned its place?
Name a small splurge you do regret. What were you really paying for?
Pick a moment money felt unreal. What happened that day?
There's an amount of money that scares you. Why exactly that amount?
Describe the last time you held cash and felt strange about it.
Name a financial decision you'd undo if it were free. What's the cost of having to live with it?
Recall a stretch where generosity felt like control. Whose was it?
Pick a piece of technology you mistrusted before adopting. What turned you?
Describe an app that has changed your behavior in a way you don't entirely like.
Name a website you've visited every day for years without thinking about why.
Recall the first email address you ever had. What does its name say about that version of you?
There's a folder on your computer you avoid opening. What's in it?
Pick a photograph on your phone you don't show anyone. Why does it stay?
Describe the way you organize your inbox. Does it match the rest of your life?
Name something you've Googled and felt embarrassed by. What were you trying to know?
Recall a notification that ruined a day. What was it?
There's a contact in your phone you can't bring yourself to delete. Whose name is it under?
Pick a username you've used for years. What did you mean by it then?
Describe a conversation you had only via text that should have been in person. What did you lose?
Name an instance of misreading the tone of a message. How did the misreading reshape the relationship?
Recall a typo someone else made that changed how you saw them. What did the slip reveal?
There's a cliché you secretly believe is true. Defend it.
Pick a saying your parents repeated. Do you repeat it now?
Describe a piece of folk wisdom that turned out to be wrong. What replaced it for you?
Name a superstition you maintain even though you don't believe in it. Why bother?
Recall an old wives' tale you tested. What happened?
There's a mythology — religious, secular, personal — you find beautiful even though you reject it. Walk into the beauty.
Pick a public ritual you find moving despite yourself. What's working on you?
Describe a religious or ritual space you stepped into as a non-believer. What did the air feel like?
Name a holiday you've stopped celebrating. What did dropping it free you from, if anything?
Recall a tradition you invented for yourself. How does it work?
Pick a milestone you missed without noticing. When did you realize?
There's a coming-of-age moment in your culture that didn't apply to you. What did you do instead?
Describe an age you still feel like internally despite the calendar. Where does the gap live?
Name an adult task you still feel unqualified for. What would qualified look like?
Recall the realization that you were the adult in the room. Were you ready?
Pick a piece of paperwork you've been avoiding. What's the avoidance protecting?
Describe a permanent administrative inconvenience in your life. Whose fault is it really?
Name a bureaucracy you've successfully outwitted. How?
Recall a queue you stood in that felt out of proportion. What did you think about while you waited?
There's a form you've filled out wrong on purpose. What were you protecting?
Pick the worst customer service experience you've had. What were you really angry about?
Describe a stretch of being a difficult customer. Were you justified?
Name an employee whose face you remember from a single transaction. What did they do?
Recall a tip you over- or under-tipped. What were you signaling?
Pick a price you remember paying that surprised you in the moment. What was the gap between expected and actual?
There's a cost you keep paying because the alternative is more annoying than expensive. What is it?
Describe a subscription you forgot you had. How long had it been running?
Name a possession you're embarrassed to own. Defend it anyway.
Recall the first object you bought with your own money. What did it teach you about money?
Pick a thing you owned that you should have given away earlier. Why did you hold on?
There's a box in your home you haven't opened in years. Open it on paper.
Describe a clean-out that felt like grief. What were you really losing?
Name a smell of a closet, drawer, or trunk that surprised you. What was in there?
Recall a piece of furniture inherited under awkward circumstances. Where does it live now?
Pick a piece of family history you don't believe but pretend to. Why is the pretense useful?
There's a relative everyone tells the same story about. What does the repetition obscure?
Describe a ceremony you sat through without understanding. What were you doing during the long parts?
Name a wedding detail you remember years later. Why has it lasted?
Recall a toast you wished hadn't happened. What was the speaker missing?
Pick an instance of bad timing that became a story. Who tells it now?
There's a story your family tells about you that you'd contest. What's your version?
Describe a nickname you outgrew or rejected. What did it cost you to let it go?
Name a word you mispronounced for years before being corrected. Who corrected you?
Recall a fact you believed until embarrassingly late. How did you find out?
Pick something you pretended to know about. Have you actually learned it since?
There's a topic you avoid in conversation. What are you protecting?
Describe an opinion you've kept off the internet for safety. What's the cost of keeping it inside?
Name a piece of news that broke wrong for you. What did the rupture feel like?
Recall a public event you remember by where you were. What was happening in your life that day?
Pick a generation you think misunderstands your generation. Steel-man their misunderstanding.
There's a year of news you'd want erased from your memory if you could. Why?
Describe a leader you respected reluctantly. Where did the respect leak in?
Name a piece of legislation that affected your daily life. What did you notice changing?
Recall the moment you became politically self-aware. What lit up first?
Pick a stance you've evolved on. Walk through the evolution honestly.
There's a thing you used to argue about that you've stopped arguing about. What did you decide wasn't worth winning?
Describe a long-standing disagreement you have with someone. What sustains it?
Name a topic you're tired of having opinions on. What would you do with the freed mental space?
Recall a meeting in which you said the wrong thing. What was the right thing, in retrospect?
Pick an act of bravery you don't take credit for. Why don't you?
There's a question you wish someone would ask you. Answer it now without being asked.
Describe a question you wish people would stop asking you. What's behind your fatigue?
Name a compliment you couldn't accept. What was wrong with it?
Recall a critique that landed and hurt and was right. What did you do with it?
Pick a piece of feedback you ignored at the time and validated later. Why did it take so long?
There's a mentor figure who would not call themselves your mentor. Describe them.
Describe an apprenticeship — formal or informal — that shaped you. Whose hands were you watching?
Name an instance where a stranger said something that changed your trajectory. What were they doing in your life that day?
Recall the last time you took notes by hand. What were you trying to slow down?
Pick a notebook or journal you still have. What's the first line?
There's a piece of writing you've never published. What's keeping it private?
Describe a draft you abandoned that you sometimes return to. Why won't it leave?
Name a sentence you've been turning over in your head for weeks. What's it close to saying?
Recall a piece of your own writing you reread and didn't recognize. What changed in you, not the page?
Pick a metaphor you use too often. What's it letting you avoid?
There's a topic you can't write about clearly because you're too close. Try anyway.
Describe an idea that has become uninteresting since you understood it. What was the heat in the not-understanding?
Name a question you used to ask that you've outgrown. What replaced it?
Recall an instance of certainty that turned out to be wrong. How does certainty feel different to you now?
Pick a belief you'd hold even with no one to share it. Why does it survive solitude?
There's a position you've held only because someone you respected held it. Have you tested it for yourself?
Describe an argument you'd lose if it ever happened. What's the structural weakness in your position?
Name a thing you do well but quietly. What would louder credit cost you?
Recall a recognition you received and didn't deserve. What did you do with it?
Pick an instance of luck mistaken for skill. Whose mistake was it?
There's an instance of skill mistaken for luck. Whose mistake was that?
Describe a competition you watched without caring about who won. What kept you watching?
Name a sport you don't follow that you wish you did. What's in your way?
Recall a game you played as a child that has informed how you work as an adult. How?
Pick a board game or card game that comes out only at specific gatherings. Whose tradition is it?
There's a physical activity that quiets your mind. Walk into it without naming the activity.
Describe a body of water you've returned to multiple times. What's different each time?
Name a hill — real or metaphorical — you've climbed enough times to know its weather. What would you tell a stranger about it?
Recall a hike that turned. What turned it?
Pick a piece of camping gear you've owned too long to replace. What's it survived?
There's a route you've walked at every life stage. What stays the same?
Describe the first city you visited alone. What was the most foreign part?
Name a country you'd return to immediately and one you wouldn't. The contrast says what?
Recall a place you got lost and didn't mind. What did being lost give you?
Pick a hotel room you remember in detail. What was in the desk drawer?
There's an airport bathroom you remember. What were you crying or laughing about?
Describe a transit moment — a layover, a delayed train, a bus stop — that became a small life. What happened inside that hour?
Name a stranger who shared food with you while traveling. What did they offer?
Recall a language barrier you crossed without language. What gestured you through?

Describe a piece of unfinished thinking you suspect is important. Where is it stuck?
There's a question you keep almost asking yourself. Ask it on paper now.
Pick a contradiction you live with daily. How does it actually work in practice?
Describe a hypocrisy you've forgiven yourself for. Why this one?
Recall an instance of catching yourself in a lie. What did you do?
Pick a kind of lie you tell often. Why this kind?
Describe a kind of truth you tell that you wish you didn't. What's the cost of the honesty?
There's a category of secret you keep well. Why are you good at it?
Pick a category of secret that always slips out of you. Why is it slippery?
Describe a piece of gossip you're proud of having stopped. What did you do?
There's an instance of gossip you regret participating in. Walk into the regret.
Pick an instance of being gossiped about you survived intact. What protected you?
Recall an episode of public embarrassment that no one else remembers. What's still vivid for you?
Pick an episode of public embarrassment you've laughed off as if it didn't count. Did it?
Describe an instance of putting your foot in your mouth and being saved. Who saved you?
There's an instance of being saved socially by someone who wasn't paying attention. Walk into the irony.
Pick an instance of saving someone socially yourself. Did they thank you?
Describe a small kindness you've watched between strangers. What was the structure of it?
Recall a kindness you delivered late. Did it still work?
Pick a kindness you'd extend if you knew the person better. Who is it for?
There's a stranger you wish you knew. What's the obstacle?
Describe a stranger you actively avoid. What's wrong with that?
Pick a person you cross the street to avoid. Why?
Recall an instance of being crossed away from. Did you know why?
Pick a piece of body language you read wrong. What did you assume?
Describe an instance of reading body language perfectly. What gave it away?
There's a stranger whose mood became your mood for a day. Walk into the contagion.
Pick an instance of being sad about something not your own. Whose grief was it?
Describe a piece of music that lifted your mood without your permission. What didn't you want lifted?
There's a sad mood you've protected. What were you working through inside it?
Pick a happy mood that scared you. What were you bracing for?
Describe an instance of joy that felt borrowed. From whom?
Recall an episode of contentment you almost missed. What pulled you back into it?
Pick a stretch of being unable to enjoy good news. What was in the way?
There's an instance of good news arriving on a bad day. What got mixed?
Describe an instance of bad news arriving on a good day. What stayed of the good?
Pick a stretch of pure relief. What were you finally not carrying?
There's a stretch of dread that didn't pay off. What did you waste worrying about?
Describe a piece of dread that paid off in slow motion. What did you see coming?
Recall an instance of trusting yourself with a small decision. What went well?
Pick a small decision you've second-guessed for years. Why this one?
Describe a stretch of not knowing what you wanted. What did you do during it?
There's a desire you've talked yourself out of. Walk back to before the talk.
Pick a want you've grown into. What was the threshold?
Recall a want that has grown smaller. What deflated it?
Pick a discipline that took effort and now feels native. When did the conversion happen?
There's a routine that took years to settle. What was it like before settling?
Describe a stretch of irregular sleep that taught you something. What was the gift?
Pick a meal pattern that has changed across your life. What's the current shape?
Recall a body change you didn't expect. How did you notice?
Describe a piece of your body you watch change. What's it telling you?
There's a piece of your body you've come to like. Was the liking earned?
Pick an instance of being told your appearance had changed. Was it accurate?
Describe an instance of seeing yourself in a photograph and not recognizing yourself. When?
Recall the first gray hair, wrinkle, or other marker. What was your first thought?
Pick an instance of feeling young that surprised you. What set it off?
Describe an instance of feeling old that surprised you. What set that off?
There's an age you'll never reach. Whose age?
Pick a person who never made it to your current age. Walk into the asymmetry.
Describe an instance of outliving an animal you loved. What did the outliving feel like?
Recall an instance of an animal outliving someone. Did the animal seem to understand?
Pick an instance of grief that surprised you in its size. Whose, and why so large?
Describe a piece of grief that arrived smaller than expected. Did you trust it?
There's a grief you've been carrying without naming. Try a name on it now.
Pick a small loss that has stuck disproportionately. Why has it lasted?
Describe a stretch of loss after which you had to relearn ordinary things. What did you have to relearn?
Recall an act of recovery you can identify. What was the first sign?
Pick a piece of healing that surprised you with its slowness. What's the slowness teaching?
There's a piece of healing that surprised you with its speed. What scared you about that?
Describe a stretch of stuckness that ended for no reason you can name. What was actually happening underneath?
Pick a turning point you didn't recognize at the time. When did it become visible?
There's a stretch of life that needs more credit than you've given it. Walk into the underrating.
Describe an instance that has become more important the further away you get from it. What grew?
Recall an instance that mattered less than you thought it would. What evaporated?
Pick a piece of gossip about your own past you've heard. Whose version was it?
Describe an event you've heard described differently by different witnesses. Whose version do you trust most?
There's a story you've never been able to verify. Why have you not asked the people who'd know?
Pick a piece of family lore you suspect is invented. What about it doesn't fit?
Describe a piece of family lore you wish was true. Why does it call to you?
Recall a piece of personal lore you've polished over the years. What's been removed in the polishing?
Pick an early ambition you'd be embarrassed to admit. Defend it from the inside.
There's a job you imagined for yourself and never pursued. What's it like in your imagination?
Describe a parallel life you've considered. What's appealing about it now?
Pick a version of yourself in another country. What does that life look like in detail?
There's a version of yourself in another era. Where would you fit, and where would you fail?
Describe a profession you've considered changing into. What's holding you?
Recall an instance of asking someone about their work that revealed more than expected. What did they tell you?
Pick a colleague you'd want as a friend if work weren't in the way. Why has the friendship not happened?
Describe a coworker who shaped you who'd be surprised to know it. What did they do?
There's a workplace politic you mishandled. What would you do differently?
Pick an instance of speaking up at work that paid off. What did you risk?
Describe an instance of staying quiet at work that paid off. What did you protect?
Recall an instance of quitting that surprised you with its speed. What flipped?
Pick an instance of staying when leaving was right. What kept you?
There's a job you stayed at too long. What did the staying teach?
Pick a piece of typography you've stared at long enough to find personality in. What letter has the most personality?
Describe an instance of running that became something more than exercise. Where were you?
There's a song you've sung alone that you'd never sing in front of anyone. Walk into it.
Recall an instance of singing with others that surprised you. What did you suddenly hear?
Pick the smell of something baking that has stayed with you. Whose oven?
Describe the smell of soil after rain, in a place specific to you. Where was the place?
There's a piece of detergent or soap whose smell is loaded for you. Whose laundry, whose hands?
Pick an act of folding — clothing, paper, anything — that calms you. What's calming about the geometry?
Describe an act of tying — laces, knots, ties — you've done thousands of times. What does the muscle memory feel like now?
Recall an instance of being unprepared that worked out. What did the unpreparedness reveal?
Pick an instance of being over-prepared and being wrong about what mattered. What was the actual ask?
There's a flash of understanding you can date. Where were you when it arrived?
Describe a piece of slow learning whose date you can't pin. What evidence do you have that it happened?
Recall the first time you taught someone something they already knew. How did you find out?
Pick a student you were that you'd want to apologize to your teacher for. What were you doing?
Describe an instance of listening so well you stopped existing in the conversation. To whom?
There's a question you've asked that opened something unexpected. What was the opening?
Pick a name you've given to a thing that didn't need a name. Why did you name it?
Describe an act of drawing — even badly — that surprised you with its meditation. What were you drawing?
Recall a tinkering session that lost the original problem. Where did you end up?
Pick a maintenance task you find improbably satisfying. What's the satisfaction's source?
There's an object in your home that gets hand-washed. Why does it deserve the care?
Describe a repair you've put off long enough that you suspect you won't make it. What's protected by the postponement?
Recall a stretch of cleaning that became thinking. What did you find under the surface, mentally?
Pick a piece of tidying that revealed something. What did you notice on the second pass?
Describe the smell of bread that triggers something specific. Whose bread?
There's a smell of garlic — frying, pickled, raw — tied to a person for you. Whose kitchen?
Pick an instance of sweat you remember by the salt. What were you doing?
Describe rain on your skin that you couldn't have skipped. Where, and why was it required?
Recall an instance of sea spray on your face. What body of water?
Pick an experience of wind in your hair you remember as a body memory. What was the speed?
There's a piece of furniture you've fallen asleep on outside of bed. What was the position?
Describe a couch that was important to you. What's its current location?
Recall a pillow that came with you across moves. Why has this one survived?
Pick a sleep position you've outgrown. When did your body protest?
Describe an instance of crying in public that didn't embarrass you. What allowed the calm?
There's an instance of crying in private that surprised you with its source. What was it actually about?
Pick an instance of joy you've kept private. Why didn't you share it?
Describe an act of singing in the car alone. What song, what road?
Recall an instance of dancing alone in your home. What music, what mood?
Pick an instance of dancing in front of others that didn't embarrass you. What was the social gravity?

Pick a stretch of walking where the destination stopped mattering. Why?
Recall the first time you understood your own body's limit. What stopped you?
There's a piece of food you eat slowly. Why this one?
Pick a piece of food you eat fast on purpose. What's the pleasure of speed?
Describe a meal you ate standing up that was perfect. What was the standing for?
Recall a snack you've eaten alone in the dark. What were you avoiding?
Pick a beverage you've stayed up late with. What were you reading or thinking?

Describe a stretch of being awake at 4 AM without purpose. What did the time feel like?
Recall a candlelit room you've been in. Whose candle?
Pick a streetlight you've stood under for a strange length of time. What were you waiting on?
Describe headlights cutting through fog. Where were you?
There's a lamp in your home with a peculiar history. What's the history?
Pick a mirror in your home you avoid in the morning. Why?
Describe a reflection in water that startled you. What did you see?
There's a reflection in a screen you've looked at and felt strange about. Walk into the strangeness.
Pick a time of day you find ugly. What's ugly about it?
Describe a time of day that flatters everything. What does that flattery cost the rest?
Recall an instance of light through a specific window at a specific time. What was the room for?
Pick a shadow you've watched move. Where were you?
There's a piece of moonlight you remember on a surface. What surface?
Describe an instance of darkness so complete it taught you something. Where?
Recall a stretch of silence so total it took on a sound of its own. Where, and what was the sound?
Pick a piece of distance — a far light, a far voice — that has stuck with you. What was at the other end?
Describe an echo you remember. What was the original sound?
There's a piece of acoustics in a specific room you remember. What was it like to talk in?
Pick an instance of overhearing yourself recorded. What did you sound like?
Describe a piece of your own laughter on tape that surprised you. What about it didn't match?
Recall a voicemail you left that you wished you could pull back. What's in it?
Pick a message you sent and then watched the typing dots on the other end for too long. What were you bracing for?
There's a thread you've muted. Whose, and what's the cost of the mute?
Describe an unread message you keep meaning to read. What's the avoidance protecting?
Recall an inbox you've reached zero on once. Did the zero feel like anything?
Pick a piece of digital clutter you've kept for sentiment. What is it?
There's a deleted file you wish you hadn't deleted. What was it?

Pick a screenshot you took years ago that still serves a purpose. What's it for?

There's a photo someone took of you that you secretly love. Why secretly?

Describe an instance of being captured on video without knowing it. What did you find out?
Recall a recording of someone's voice you can return to. Whose?
Pick a piece of music you can't play because of who introduced you to it. Why hasn't time helped?

Describe an instance of writing a postcard you almost mailed. To whom?
Recall the last thing you wrote that wasn't to or for anyone. What was it for, then?
Pick a piece of writing on a wall — graffiti, sign, sticker — you've stopped to read. What did it say?
There's a sentence in someone else's handwriting that has stayed with you. Whose hand?

Pick an instance of seeing your own handwriting and feeling like a stranger. When?

Pick a stranger online you've felt close to. What's the proximity made of?
Describe a community online that has shaped you. What's the structure of belonging there?
